name: Props Table
summary: Renders a component's Custom Elements Manifest (#653) as a sortable API reference table — attributes, properties, events, slots, CSS custom-properties and parts. Ratified by #626 Fork 1 (sub-decision): the component API-table renderer consumes the CEM emit rather than a bespoke metadata shape, so one manifest feeds the table, the Technical Configurator args panel, and autodocs. Powers the autodocs capability in the Web Docs catalog surface (#627). Not a new control standard — a derived view over the custom-elements-manifest protocol.

customElementsManifest.pattern: Custom Elements Manifest (custom-elements.json, schema 2.1.0) as the input contract
customElementsManifest.adoption: Reads a CEM declaration (or the whole manifest keyed by tag) and projects its members/attributes/events/slots/cssProperties into table rows — no bespoke metadata shape.

ariaSort.pattern: aria-sort on the sortable column headers (the table is a reference, sortable by name/kind)
ariaSort.adoption: Sorting reuses the collection-operations intent so the column-sort affordance matches every other WE table.

consumesCemNotBespoke.description: Input is the Custom Elements Manifest, not a props-table-specific schema
consumesCemNotBespoke.rationale: #626 Fork 1: one CEM (#653) feeds the props-table, the Technical Configurator args panel, and autodocs — a derived view, never a second metadata source to keep in sync.

derivedViewNotAStandard.description: A rendering of the CEM protocol, not a new control standard
derivedViewNotAStandard.rationale: The contract is the manifest; this block is one presentation of it, so it adds no new conformance surface — it composes collection-operations for the sortable table.

gracefulPartialManifest.description: Sections with no manifest data are omitted, not shown empty
gracefulPartialManifest.rationale: The CEM emit is a projection of whatever fields a block carries (#653) — many blocks have events but no slots yet, so the table renders only the populated member kinds.
